$description: Lumina AI Visibility Platform — Component Manifest (Atomic Design)

categories.name: Atoms
categories.description: Low-level UI primitives based on shadcn/ui with CVA variants. No domain knowledge, no API calls, no feature imports. May import from lib/.

components.name: Button
components.notes: Primary action button. Sizes via separate CVA dimension: default, sm, lg, icon.

components.name: Input
components.notes: Text input field. inputSize variants: sm, default, lg.

components.name: Checkbox
components.notes: Checkbox input. checkboxSize variants: sm, default, lg.

components.name: Badge
components.notes: Small label badge for status, categories, and counts.

components.name: Tooltip
components.notes: Hover tooltip for additional context on elements.

components.name: Card
components.notes: Container card with variant-based styling.

components.name: Separator
components.notes: Visual separator line. Orientation managed via CVA.

components.name: Skeleton
components.notes: Loading placeholder that mimics content shape while data loads.

components.name: Progress
components.notes: Progress bar with semantic color variants. progressSize variants: sm, default, lg.

components.name: Alert
components.notes: Inline alert banner for contextual messages and warnings.

components.name: ScrollArea
components.notes: Custom scrollable container with styled scrollbar.

components.name: Label
components.notes: Form label element. labelSize variants: sm, default, lg.

components.name: Textarea
components.notes: Multi-line text input with default and error states.

components.name: Select
components.notes: Custom styled dropdown select built on Radix UI Select. Supports icons per item, placeholder, size variants, and check indicator. Linear-inspired design.

components.name: RadioGroup
components.notes: Radio button group for single-selection options.

components.name: Switch
components.notes: Toggle switch for boolean settings.

components.name: Avatar
components.notes: User avatar with image or initials fallback in multiple sizes.

components.name: Popover
components.notes: Floating popover container for menus, tooltips, and selects.

components.name: Dialog
components.notes: Modal dialog for focused user interactions.

components.name: Sheet
components.notes: Slide-over drawer panel anchored to a screen edge.

components.name: DropdownMenu
components.notes: Contextual dropdown menu triggered by a button or icon.

components.name: Command
components.notes: Command palette and searchable list for quick actions and search.

components.name: Tabs
components.notes: Tab navigation for switching between content panels.

components.name: Accordion
components.notes: Collapsible accordion sections for progressive disclosure.

components.name: AlertDialog
components.notes: Confirmation dialog requiring explicit user acknowledgement.

components.name: Toast
components.notes: Ephemeral notification toast that appears and auto-dismisses.

components.name: SidebarNavItem
components.notes: Individual nav item with icon, label, and active state.

components.name: InlineEdit
components.notes: Click-to-edit text field. Displays as styled text, transforms to Input on click. Commits on Enter/blur, cancels on Escape.

components.name: BreadcrumbNav
components.notes: Breadcrumb navigation component.

categories.name: Molecules
categories.description: Composed components built from 2+ atoms. No domain knowledge, no API calls, no feature imports. May import from atoms/ and lib/.

components.name: PageHeader
components.notes: Page title, breadcrumbs, and optional actions.

components.name: BrandSelector
components.notes: Phase 4 v3 multi-select dropdown — two sections (Tracked brand[s] / All brands) each with a master checkbox + per-entity rows, client-side substring search, click-outside dismiss. Controlled — caller owns the selectedKeys (formatted as `<entityType>:<id>`). Used by Insights / Trackers / Prompts / Domains / SourceUrls / Competitors screens; the Workspace Overview moved to EntityScopeToggle, deriving selectedKeys from a simpler 3-option scope chip.

components.name: DateRangePicker
components.notes: Peec-style date range picker for the Workspace Overview top bar. Preset list (Last 7/14/30/90/180/365 days, All time, Reset) on the left + react-day-picker range calendar on the right. Selection state modeled as a tagged union (preset|custom|all) so rolling-window presets don't burst the React Query cache between renders; exports resolveDateRange + serializeDateRangeSelection helpers consumed by useWorkspace{Overview,Competitive,Depth}.

components.name: LensChipRow
components.notes: Row of 6 chips — one per Visibility Lens (Discovery, Buying Intent, Competitor Comparison, Sentiment & Trust, Citation Visibility, Content Gaps) — that doubles as both lens selector AND section anchor on the Workspace Overview. Default state: all 6 codes in selectedCodes (every chip pressed); clicking a chip narrows to just that one via onChange (and emits onActivate so the host can scrollTo the section). Clicking the only-selected chip a second time toggles back to all 6. Each chip shows a lens-specific icon + a mention-count badge from `/api/overview/lens-counts`. The host filters its rendered sections by the same selection set, so isolating one lens collapses the page to just that section.

components.name: DateGranularityToggle
components.notes: Day / Week / Month aggregation toggle that lives next to the DateRangePicker. Now wrapped by DateRangeGranularityPicker on the Workspace Overview to collapse both controls into a single trigger; still used standalone elsewhere.

components.name: DateRangeGranularityPicker
components.notes: Single compact trigger that combines the date range + chart granularity for the Workspace Overview page header. Trigger reads 'Last 30 days · Weekly'; popover stacks a D/W/M segmented control above the standard preset list + react-day-picker calendar. Replaces the previous two-control card (DateGranularityToggle stacked over DateRangePicker) to shrink the controls strip from 4 rows to 2.

components.name: FiltersPopover
components.notes: Compact Filters trigger + slide-down panel that consolidates several selectors behind one chip on the Workspace Overview controls strip. Exports FiltersPopover + FiltersPopoverRow for labeled-row composition. Trigger shows a primary count badge when any filter is applied; the panel does NOT auto-close on click within itself so each child's own popover (TopicSelector, ProductSelector, etc.) can open without conflict — close on Escape, the X button, or a re-click of the trigger.

components.name: EntityScopeToggle
components.notes: 3-option segmented chip (All / Tracked / Top 5) that replaces the multi-select BrandSelector on the Workspace Overview controls strip. Caller derives the actual selectedKeys list from this value + workspace data — `top5` ranks by mentionCount from the competitive payload. The sidebar tracker scope owns the brand filter; this control just picks the entity slice.

components.name: OnboardingStepper
components.notes: Compact pill-dot progress indicator used across the first-tracker onboarding flow (prompt-generation + scan-progress screens today; future onboarding steps reuse the same shape). The active step gets an elongated pill; reached steps are filled circles; remaining steps are muted.

components.name: RotatingMessage
components.notes: Cycles through a list of messages every N ms with a short fade. Sits on long-running onboarding screens (prompt generation, scan progress) so the user has something to read while they wait — also used to sneak product education into otherwise blank wait time.

components.name: MarketSelector
components.notes: Dropdown chip for filtering the Workspace Overview by Market. Body is split into per-brand sections (one header per brand with that brand's markets underneath) so the user can see which brand each market belongs to. Selection is still name-based, so a market name shared across brands toggles in both sections at once. Multi-select with substring search and inline mention-count chip per row. Selection threads as `?marketNames=` through the three overview endpoints; the BE filters PromptRuns whose Prompt has any matching PromptMarket row.

components.name: ProductSelector
components.notes: Dropdown chip for filtering the Workspace Overview by Product. Body is split into per-brand sections (one header per brand with that brand's products underneath) so the user can see which brand each product belongs to. Selection is still name-based, so a product name shared across brands toggles in both sections at once. Multi-select with substring search and inline mention-count chip per row. Selection threads as `?productNames=` through the three overview endpoints; the BE filters PromptRuns whose Prompt has any matching PromptProduct row.

components.name: TopicSelector
components.notes: Dropdown chip for filtering the Workspace Overview by Topic. Body is split into per-brand sections (one header per brand with that brand's topics underneath) so the user can see which brand each topic belongs to. Selection is still name-based, so a topic name shared across brands toggles in both sections at once. Multi-select with checkboxes, substring search, optional per-topic mention-count chip. Empty array sentinel reads as 'All topics'. Selection threads as `?topicNames=` through the three overview endpoints.

components.name: AudienceSelector
components.notes: Dropdown chip for filtering the Workspace Overview by Audience. Body is split into per-brand sections (one header per brand with that brand's audiences underneath) so the user can see which brand each audience belongs to. Selection is still name-based, so an audience name shared across brands toggles in both sections at once. Multi-select with substring search and inline mention-count chip per row. Selection threads as `?audienceNames=` through the three overview endpoints; the BE filters PromptRuns whose Prompt has any matching PromptAudience row.

components.name: TrustSignalsPill
components.notes: Informational pill in the unified Workspace Overview filter bar. Trigger shows the workspace-wide unique trust-signal count; clicking opens a popover that lists the signals grouped per brand (matching the per-brand sectioning used by the four real filter dropdowns). Does NOT filter — per the Phase-3 decision, trust signals are scoring inputs, not prompt-level filter dimensions (no PromptTrustSignal table exists). Sits alongside the real filter pills so the user has visibility into the dimension without implying functionality we don't support.

components.name: CollapsibleCard
components.notes: Drop-in wrapper that combines Card + ChartCardHeader + a collapse chevron toggle in the header. Used across every analytics card on /overview so users can hide charts they don't currently care about. Collapse state is per-card local — does not persist across page reloads yet.

components.name: ChartCardHeader
components.notes: Compact header for chart cards on the Workspace Overview. Leading primary-tinted icon badge + neutral-900 title + optional info tooltip (delegates to InfoTooltip when the `tooltip` prop is non-empty) + optional right-side actions slot + optional trailing collapse chevron when `onToggleCollapsed` is wired (used by CollapsibleCard). Tooltip body is plain text resolved by the caller from the content layer — the molecule stays a pure UI primitive so locale switching happens upstream.

components.name: SourceTypeDropdown
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 2 editable SourceType picker. Wraps Select with the 12-value taxonomy from source_types reference table; tooltips surface the ADR-003 definition for each value. Accepts source types as a prop so the caller controls where the data comes from (hook-driven in the Source/Citation view).

components.name: LoadingPage
components.notes: Full-page loading state with skeleton layout.

components.name: ErrorPage
components.notes: Full-page error state with message and retry action.

components.name: PageContent
components.notes: Content wrapper with max-width and padding.

components.name: EmptyStatePanel
components.notes: Contextual empty state panel with guidance and next steps.

components.name: PartialResultsWarning
components.notes: Warning banner displayed when data is incomplete or partial.

components.name: PageNotFound
components.notes: 404 page not found screen.

components.name: Stepper
components.notes: Reusable wizard stepper with horizontal steps (per-step icons or numbers), connecting lines, checkmarks for completed steps, and Back/Next navigation.

components.name: SectionHeader
components.notes: Linear-style section header: optional leading icon chip, tight-tracked title + muted description, and right-aligned meta/actions slots.

components.name: AliasEditor
components.notes: Chip-style 'also known as' editor: existing aliases render as removable Badge chips, ending in a subtle '+' icon button. Clicking '+' reveals an input (Enter to add and keep input open, Escape or empty blur to collapse back). Case-insensitive duplicate prevention. Used by the discovery wizard for Brand.Aliases on BrandIdentity and per-candidate Product/Competitor aliases on SuggestionCard.

components.name: MetricCategoryLayout
components.notes: Categorized dashboard scaffold: optional status + controls strips, sticky underline-style category nav, stacked sections. IntersectionObserver-driven scroll-spy highlights the section in view; clicking a tab scrolls + syncs the URL hash for shareable deep links. The default underline nav can be replaced via the `renderNav` prop (called with activeId + scrollTo) — the Workspace Overview uses this escape hatch to render its lens chip row as the section nav. `showSectionHeadings` toggles the per-section h2 above each section body.

components.name: InfoTooltip
components.notes: Single ⓘ + hover/focus tooltip used by every chart-card title and KPI tile label across the app. Trigger is a focusable `<span role=button>` rather than a real `<button>` so it can be safely nested inside a clickable parent (HeroTile renders as a button when its tile is clickable). Click + keyDown propagation stopped so activating the icon doesn't also fire the parent's onClick. `body` defaults to `INFO_TOOLTIP_PLACEHOLDER` ("Description coming soon.") so callers can drop the icon in before real copy is authored. Consumed by ChartCardHeader (renders only when its `tooltip` prop is non-empty), ScanResultsScreen ChartTitle + MetricTile, and WorkspaceOverviewScreen HeroTile.

components.name: HeroRowCompact
components.notes: Compact 4-tile KPI row: Queries / Mentions / Citations / Brand mention rate. Each tile shows the absolute value plus a percent delta chip vs the previous-period hero (up = success-green, down = error-red, zero-baseline-but-nonzero = 'New' badge). Returns no delta when previousHero is null, when both periods are zero, or when the delta rounds to 0%. Used by TrackerHubScreen's Overview tab for the at-a-glance single-tracker summary. Larger multi-row hero treatments live in WorkspaceOverviewScreen (separate HeroTile composition with click-to-scroll drill-down) and are deliberately not consolidated since they carry tooltip + invertDelta affordances that do not apply to this compact variant.

components.name: AiNarrativeSection
components.notes: On-demand LLM-authored narrative panel. Single Generate button until clicked, then surfaces the model response inline with a 'via {platform}' byline and a Regenerate affordance. Hidden entirely when hasData=false so empty trackers/workspaces do not show a button that calls a paid endpoint to summarize nothing. Mutation state lives in the underlying hook (useGenerateAiNarrative in hooks/useAiNarrative.ts) so filter/scope changes do not blow the result away; the user clicks Regenerate explicitly. Used by TrackerHubScreen Overview tab (single-tracker scope, threading trackerIds={[trackerId]}).

components.name: EntityTrendDrillDown
components.notes: Inline drill-down panel for one row in a ranking-style top-entities table. Renders the per-scan trend line (BrandMentionRate for tracked brands, MentionRate for competitors) using the workspace overview series payload, or a soft hint when the entity has no numeric points (single scan, all nulls, missing series). Pure presentational component; the entity-to-series lookup lives in lib/entityTrend.ts (findEntityTrend) so this .tsx only exports a component and stays inside Vite react-refresh boundary. Used by TrackerHubScreen TopEntities card for click-to-expand drill-down.

components.name: Breadcrumb
components.notes: Compact navigation breadcrumb — `Workspace › Brand › Tracker › Scan`. Items with `to` render as TanStack Links (with optional `params` for dynamic routes); items without `to` and the final item render as plain text with `aria-current=page` on the tail. Items with `label === undefined` render a small skeleton segment so the trail keeps its layout while async parent labels resolve. The `to` prop is cast to `never` internally because the molecule is generic — consumers trade route-string type safety for a uniform API. Used by management pages and scan detail pages once wired up in the navigation phase.

components.name: Tabs
components.notes: Underlined tab strip with a single visible panel; active tab is mirrored to the URL `?tab=` query param (configurable via `paramName`). Distinct from MetricCategoryLayout — Tabs hide inactive panels entirely, which is the right pattern for hubs (Tracker hub) where one panel is in focus at a time. The URL sync uses `history.replaceState` so back/forward leaves the page rather than unwinding tab clicks, and other query params (notably `?trackers=` analytics scope) are preserved when the tab changes. Keyboard support: ArrowLeft/Right cycle with wrap-around, Home/End jump to first/last, focus follows selection. ARIA: role=tablist/tab/tabpanel with aria-selected, aria-controls, aria-labelledby wired correctly.

components.name: TrackerSelector
components.notes: Top-of-sidebar analytics scope selector. Multi-select checkbox tree — brands group their trackers; all-on by default. Tri-state master ('All trackers') and per-brand checkboxes with standard semantics (checked → click → uncheck; unchecked or indeterminate → click → select all). Selection state lives in the URL `?trackers=` param via `useTrackerScope` — the molecule itself is stateless w.r.t. URL and accepts `scope` + `onScopeChange` as props for unit-testability. Trackers with `hasScans === false` render greyed with a 'No scans yet' hint and remain checkable. Search filters by brand name OR tracker name; an empty workspace renders a 'No trackers yet · Add a brand' state. Click-outside + Escape close the dropdown. Trigger pill label adapts: 'All trackers (N)' / 'None selected' / single tracker name / 'N of M trackers'. Pure helpers (`allTrackerIds`, `isTrackerSelected`, `computeWorkspaceState`, `computeBrandState`, `toggleTracker`, `toggleBrand`, `toggleWorkspace`, `triggerLabel`) are exported so consumers can test selection math in isolation.

components.name: ComingSoon
components.notes: Placeholder body for routes that are reachable but not yet built. Used during the navigation IA rollout (workspace-wide /prompts, /sources/domains, /sources/urls, /competitors, /insights) — routes register before their screens exist so direct URLs + sidebar links don't 404. Consistent visual across the placeholder set so the gap reads as 'in flight' rather than 'broken'. Accepts optional icon (rendered in primary-tinted badge), description, and a `beta` flag that pins a small BETA pill next to the title.

components.name: ConfirmDeleteDialog
components.notes: Type-to-confirm destructive-action dialog. Used by the brand + tracker delete flows: the user must type the exact name of the resource (case-sensitive, exact match — no trim) to enable the destructive button. Closing via X, backdrop, or Cancel resets the typed text so re-opening shows a blank input. Auto-focuses the input on open and accepts an `isDeleting` flag that swaps the button label to 'Deleting…' while the parent mutation is in flight. The dialog never auto-closes on confirm — the caller controls closing after the mutation settles.

components.name: EditableChipList
components.notes: Shared chip-list editor for entity-backed brand dimensions (audiences, markets, products, topics, competitors, trust signals). Pure presentation — items + addLabel/addPlaceholder/emptyLabel/removeAriaSingular labels + onAdd/onRemove callbacks + isAdding/pendingRemoveId/serverError state come from the parent. Internally handles the typed-input state, trim + case-insensitive duplicate guard against the current items, and Enter-to-submit. Server errors are surfaced through `serverError`; the molecule never reads from React Query.

categories.name: Organisms
categories.description: Complex, often stateful sections. May compose atoms and molecules. May use shared hooks for navigation/layout state. No feature imports.

components.name: AppShell
components.notes: Main application shell with sidebar + topbar + content area.

components.name: Sidebar
components.notes: App-shell left sidebar. Top: app name + TrackerSelector pill (always visible, even on management pages where it's inert). Below: three categorized sections — ANALYTICS (Overview/Prompts/Sources › Domains/Sources › URLs/Competitors/Insights · BETA/Scans), MANAGE (Brands and Trackers — both pointing at the brand list pre-step-6, with Trackers carrying a #trackers hash for the future expand-trackers-on-/brands behavior), and SETTINGS (Workspace, Profile — placeholder routes). Step 3 of the navigation rollout. The TrackerSelector receives empty static fixtures until step 4 wires a real data hook for brand-grouped trackers.

components.name: ErrorBoundary
components.notes: React error boundary with fallback UI and retry action.

components.name: TopBar
components.notes: Top bar with workspace switcher, search, and user menu.

categories.name: Data Display
categories.description: Components for presenting data, metrics, statuses, and loading states in structured visual formats.

components.name: DataTable
components.notes: Generic data table with sorting, filtering, and pagination.

components.name: MetricCard
components.notes: Single metric display with label, value, and trend indicator.

components.name: StatusBadge
components.notes: Status indicator with color-coded dot mapped to status tokens.

components.name: SeverityBadge
components.notes: Severity level indicator mapped to severity design tokens.

components.name: PriorityBadge
components.notes: Priority level indicator with semantic color coding.

components.name: ConfidenceIndicator
components.notes: Confidence percentage display with visual progress bar.

components.name: EmptyState
components.notes: Empty state placeholder with icon, title, description, and CTA.

components.name: LoadingSkeleton
components.notes: Page-level skeleton layout for loading states.

categories.name: Charts
categories.description: Chart wrapper components built on Recharts (the same engine Tremor uses) for consistent data visualization styling.

components.name: BarChartWrapper
components.notes: Recharts BarChart wrapper. Single-series; takes a prepared {label, value, color?}[] array. Supports horizontal (default) + vertical layout, optional maxValue (pin to [0, 1] for rates), and a formatValue callback. Returns null on empty data.

components.name: LineChartWrapper
components.notes: Recharts LineChart wrapper. Single-series (data prop) and multi-series (series prop) modes. Multi-series renders a legend below the chart with a 6-color fallback palette. Supports reverseY for rank-shaped series so rank 1 sits at the top.

components.name: RadarChartWrapper
components.notes: Recharts RadarChart wrapper. Takes {axis, value}[]; renders null when fewer than 3 axes (radar requires >=3). Used in the mention-distribution radar.

components.name: DonutChartWrapper
components.notes: Recharts Pie wrapper styled as a thin donut. Takes {id, label, value, color}[] — caller owns the palette so the same entity reads as the same color across renders. Renders an inline legend with per-slice percent shares (caller can disable via showLegend=false).

components.name: HeatmapWrapper
components.notes: Custom CSS-grid heatmap (no chart library). Rows x cols matrix with intensity-mapped cells anchored to the primary token; sparse cell payload, empty cells fall back to neutral-100. Used by the topic coverage heatmap.

components.name: SentimentDonut
components.notes: Sentiment distribution donut. Composes DonutChartWrapper with the fixed sentiment-value color map (success/warning/error/neutral) so charts read consistently across scans. The pure slice-shape transform (sentimentSlices) is exported for unit testing.

categories.name: Feature — Discovery
categories.description: Components for the website discovery and crawl flow, including AI-suggested pages, topics, and manual fallback entry.

components.name: DiscoveryConfirmationScreen
components.notes: 5-step discovery confirmation wizard. Orchestrates wizard state, re-suggestion API call at step 3→4 transition, and final confirmation.

components.name: DiscoverySection
components.notes: Collapsible section within the discovery confirmation screen. Supports per-lens refresh with onRefresh/refreshesRemaining/isRefreshing props.

components.name: SuggestionCard
components.notes: Individual crawl suggestion card for a page or topic.

components.name: ConfidenceTag
components.notes: AI confidence level tag with color-coded severity.

components.name: ManualFallbackPrompt
components.notes: Manual entry prompt shown when crawl yields low results.

components.name: CrawlProgressIndicator
components.notes: Real-time crawl progress indicator with live updates via SignalR.

components.name: DiscoveryProgressScreen
components.notes: Discovery progress screen shown during crawl execution.

components.name: DiscoveryStepProgress
components.notes: Step-by-step progress indicator for the discovery workflow.

components.name: AddCustomItemForm
components.notes: Form for manually adding custom items with optional type dropdown (Select with icons).

components.name: WizardStepBrandIdentity
components.notes: Wizard step 1: brand profile card with inline-editable industry, category, and positioning fields.

components.name: WizardStepProducts
components.notes: Wizard step 2: single DiscoverySection for products with select/deselect/add-custom controls.

components.name: WizardStepAudiencesMarkets
components.notes: Wizard step 3: two DiscoverySection instances for audiences and markets.

components.name: WizardStepCompetitiveLandscape
components.notes: Wizard step 4: three DiscoverySection instances for topics, competitors, and trust signals. Shows loading overlay during re-suggestion.

components.name: WizardStepReview
components.notes: Wizard step 5: interactive review of all selections with removable/editable chips, inline add, undo toast, and edit-section deep links.

categories.name: Feature — Brands
categories.description: Components for brand management including creation, editing, and brand-level configuration.

components.name: AddBrandForm
components.notes: Form for adding a new brand with name and website URL.

components.name: BrandList
components.notes: Brand picker: lists onboarded brands with discovery status, links to each brand's profile page, and an Add Brand action.

components.name: BrandDiscoveryScreen
components.notes: Workspace-level /brand-discovery command center. Lists brands with latest discovery status, pages crawled, last run, summary tiles, empty/error states, and CTAs into add-brand, continue-discovery, or brand-profile flows.

components.name: BrandProfileScreen
components.notes: Read-only brand profile view at /brands/$brandId/profile — the canonical landing page for a single brand. Renders identity fields (description, industry, category, positioning, website), aliases as chips, and one section per dimension (products, audiences, markets, topics, competitors, trust signals) showing the items as chips. Edits route through the existing discovery wizard via a 'Re-run discovery' CTA until proper PATCH endpoints land; an inline notice surfaces that today's edit path is the wizard. Sources data from useBrand + useDiscoveryResults (which returns the full DiscoveryResultsDto).

categories.name: Feature — Tracker
categories.description: Components for creating, editing, and managing visibility trackers including prompt configuration and platform selection.

components.name: TrackerHubScreen
components.notes: Tabbed hub for a single Visibility Tracker at /brands/$brandId/trackers/$trackerId — the one 'hub-with-tabs' survivor in the navigation IA rework (everything else is flattened into the sidebar). Tabs: Overview (placeholder until MetricCategoryLayout is scoped per-tracker) · Schedule (real cadence/timezone/platforms read-only) · Prompts (read-only list, capped at 50 for the hub view) · Lenses (placeholder until a per-tracker lens endpoint lands) · Scans (workspace scan list filtered client-side via useTrackerScans). Header shows breadcrumb (Brands › Brand › Tracker), tracker name, cadence + platform chips, 'Run scan now' button (v1: button + inline status; drawer with live progress is a follow-up polish), and 'Edit' link to the edit route. Tab state synced to ?tab= via the Tabs molecule.

components.name: TrackerEditScreen
components.notes: Tracker edit screen at /brands/$brandId/trackers/$trackerId/edit. Schedule section is the only fully-editable surface today (cadence + timezone + platforms via useConfigureTrackerSchedule, saves WITHOUT kicking off a scan — distinct from the create-flow TrackerScheduleScreen which activates + scans). Prompts section renders a count + link to the wizard for actual prompt management. Lenses section is a ComingSoon placeholder pending a per-tracker lens-edit endpoint. Sticky bottom save bar appears only when the schedule form is dirty (compared field-by-field against the setup snapshot); shows 'Saving…' during mutation, 'Saved.' on success, 'Save failed — try again.' on error. Discard reverts local state to the setup snapshot.

components.name: TrackersScreen
components.notes: Workspace-level /trackers page. Replaces the product scaffold with a data-backed tracker inventory using the existing workspace tracker list endpoint. Shows summary tiles, brand coverage cards, an empty state, and a sortable table linking each tracker to its hub and brand profile.

components.name: TrackerNameEditor
components.notes: Inline editable tracker name field.

components.name: PromptCard
components.notes: Individual prompt display card with metadata and actions.

components.name: PromptReviewScreen
components.notes: Prompt review: collapsible per-Visibility-Check cards (auto-generates on first load), allocation bar, per-check add-custom + regenerate, and confirm. Confirm advances to the platform/cadence screen.

components.name: PromptLensGroup
components.notes: Collapsible card for one Visibility Lens (description, count, per-check regenerate): tinted prompt rows with AI/human source icon, multi-line inline-editable text, per-prompt remove, and an inline add-custom form.

components.name: PromptGenerationProgress
components.notes: Wait-screen rendered by PromptReviewScreen while the LLM crafts the initial prompt set. Mirrors the visual language of ScanProgressScreen (5-dot stepper at step 4, three-stage icon row with a pulsing middle stage, rotating product-awareness messages) so the multi-second wait reads as 'the system is working' rather than 'the system is stuck'. Falls back to a generic title when the brand name isn't loaded yet.

components.name: TrackerScheduleScreen
components.notes: Platform + cadence + timezone setup after prompt confirm (card with header/section icons, selectable platform tiles, scan-check estimate). Timezone defaults to the browser zone. Activate (Draft -> Active) immediately runs the first scan.

components.name: ScanProgressScreen
components.notes: Live scan progress: polls the latest scan run every 2s, shows a progress bar (completed/total checks), and a completed state with the answer count.

components.name: PlatformSelector
components.notes: Multi-select control for choosing target AI platforms.

components.name: CadenceSelector
components.notes: Scan frequency selector for scheduling tracker runs.

components.name: PromptRegenerateMenu
components.notes: Dropdown menu with AI prompt regeneration options.

components.name: TrackerStatusBadge
components.notes: Tracker lifecycle status indicator badge.

components.name: ReadyToCreateTrackerScreen
components.notes: Ready-to-create screen: editable suggested tracker name + coverage summary from the setup-preview endpoint; calls POST /brands/{id}/trackers.

categories.name: Feature — Settings
categories.description: Components for workspace and account-level settings surfaces.

components.name: WorkspaceSettingsScreen
components.notes: Workspace settings overview at /workspace. Uses a local settings hook backed by brandsApi and trackersApi to show workspace usage summary, read-only profile/team/notification/integration sections, empty usage hint, and disabled v1 account-management actions.

components.name: ProfileSettingsScreen
components.notes: Profile settings page at /settings/profile. Replaces the ComingSoon shell with read-only identity, preferences, notification, and access sections plus disabled v1 actions until account/profile endpoints are available.

categories.name: Feature — Scan
categories.description: Components for scan execution, real-time progress monitoring, and scan result summaries.

components.name: ScanProgressScreen
components.notes: Real-time scan execution progress screen with live updates.

components.name: ScanCheckCounter
components.notes: Animated counter displaying completed scan checks.

components.name: PlatformStatusCard
components.notes: Per-platform scan status card showing progress and state.

components.name: RotatingProgressMessage
components.notes: Rotating motivational status messages displayed during scan execution.

components.name: ScanSummaryHeader
components.notes: Post-scan summary statistics bar with key metrics.

components.name: ScanStatusBanner
components.notes: Contextual banner showing current scan status.

categories.name: Feature — Findings & Actions
categories.description: Components for displaying AI visibility findings, evidence summaries, and recommended content actions.

components.name: FindingCard
components.notes: Individual finding summary card with severity and confidence.

components.name: ContentActionCard
components.notes: Content action recommendation card with actionable steps.

components.name: FindingDetailDrawer
components.notes: Full finding details displayed in a right-side drawer.

components.name: ContentActionDetailDrawer
components.notes: Full content action details displayed in a drawer.

components.name: EvidenceSummaryBlock
components.notes: AI evidence summary block with source citations.

categories.name: Feature — Reporting
categories.description: Components for tracker dashboards, performance tables, evidence views, and scan run history.

components.name: ScanBreadcrumb
components.notes: Thin wrapper around `Breadcrumb` for the scan detail surfaces. Fetches the scan summary via `useScanResults` (React-Query-cached so it shares the cache entry with pages that already call it) and renders the canonical trail `Brands › {Brand} › {Tracker} › {currentLabel}`. Brand label links to `/brands/$id/profile`, tracker label links to `/brands/$id/trackers/$id`. While the summary loads the brand and tracker rows render skeleton segments so the trail keeps its layout. Used by ScanResultsScreen, ScanSourcesScreen, ScanTopicsScreen, ScanTopicDetailScreen, ScanCompetitorsScreen, ScanCompetitorDetailScreen, ScanClaimsScreen.

components.name: RecommendationsScreen
components.notes: Data-backed /recommendations workbench. Derives v1 recommendation rows from existing workspace overview and competitive read models: factual claims, risk flags, weak topic visibility, competitive gaps, not-mentioned rate, and first-mention prominence. Includes date range, Status/Impact/Lens filter pills with clear action, summary tiles that reflect the filtered queue, prioritized action table, empty state, and right-side details drawer.

components.name: LensesScreen
components.notes: Data-backed /lenses page. Replaces the product scaffold with the six Visibility Lenses, summary tiles from workspace overview, per-lens mention counts from /api/overview/lens-counts, health status badges, and links into each lens detail route.

components.name: LensDetailScreen
components.notes: Data-backed /lenses/$lensId detail page. Resolves route slugs to Visibility Lens codes and queries workspace overview with a single lens filter. Renders lens KPIs, signal cards, top-entity table, empty state, and backlink to /lenses.

components.name: TopicsScreen
components.notes: Data-backed /topics page. Derives workspace topic opportunity rows from overview.topicOwnership, prioritizing coverage gaps before contested and owned topics. Includes summary tiles, date range control, sortable DataTable, empty state, and a right-side topic opportunity drawer.

components.name: ClaimsRisksScreen
components.notes: Data-backed /claims-risks page. Uses workspace overview factual claims, risk flags, and known-for attributes to render risk summary tiles, a sortable claim review table, risk themes, known-for themes, workflow status counts, and a right-side claim review drawer.

components.name: ReportsScreen
components.notes: Dedicated /reports page for the agency/client reporting workflow. Renders report summary tiles, create-report section checklist, template chooser, report-history empty state, and disabled v1 actions for create/schedule/export until report generation endpoints land.

components.name: CompetitorsScreen
components.notes: Workspace-wide competitor ranks at /competitors. Merges `mentionDistribution` + `recommendationRates` from `useWorkspaceCompetitive` (already scope-aware after step 5) into one sortable table — columns: # · Entity · Mentions (with proportional bar) · Share of voice · Recommendation rate. Tracked brands highlighted with a 'You' badge + soft primary background; the rank-1 non-tracked-brand row gets a 'Leader' badge. Pure `mergeEntityRows` helper exported for unit testing. Scope follows the sidebar's TrackerSelector.

components.name: SourcesScreen
components.notes: Workspace-wide citation source view at /sources. Single page with a Domains ↔ URLs view toggle (replaces the prior /sources/domains and /sources/urls split). Canonical page shell — title-only PageHeader, MetricCategoryLayout with Hero KPIs (Citations · Domains · URLs · Top type) + sticky single-row controls strip (lens chips · view toggle · DateRangePicker · FiltersPopover with Topics/Products/Markets/Audiences/Models/Sentiment) + one section that swaps tables on view flip. All seven canonical filter dimensions thread through both useWorkspaceDomains + useWorkspaceUrls; the BE narrows the underlying citation set via PromptRun-level EXISTS subqueries (sentiment narrows via Mention join). Source classification mutates the underlying Source, so a flip on a URL row updates every URL backed by the same Source after the cache invalidates. Pure `filterActive`, `countByType`, and `deriveHero` helpers exported.

components.name: PromptsScreen
components.notes: Workspace-wide AI Questions surface at /ai-questions (legacy /prompts redirects). Lists active AI questions across selected trackers with quota badge, add-question dialog, search, date range, lens chips, and FiltersPopover dimensions for Topics, Products & Services, Markets, Audiences, Models, and Sentiment. Status strip includes KPI tiles, visibility distribution, and a compact questions-by-lens summary with count, mention rate, average position, and weak or missing visibility issues. Lens sections render tracked-question tables with AI question, topics, audience, market, country, tracker, platforms, visibility, sentiment, mentions, activity, derived status, inline edit/remove, and row-click PromptAnswerHistoryDrawer.

components.name: ScanResultsScreen
components.notes: Slice (d) reporting page. Composes the full Scan Results view from GET /api/scans/{id}/results — Summary + CoreMetrics + SentimentDonut + top cited sources bar + per-Platform/Lens/Topic/Competitor bar charts. Charts go through the shared /components/charts/ wrappers per ARCH-003 (no direct recharts imports here).

components.name: ScanListScreen
components.notes: Reporting Scan History page at /scan-history. Shows operational summary metrics (last completed scan, completed checks, success rate, failed runs) plus a cross-tracker scan runs table linking each row to /scans/{id}/results. Consumes GET /api/scans (capped at 100 rows); /scans redirects here while scan detail routes stay under /scans/{id}.

components.name: ScanSourcesScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 2 Source/Citation view at /scans/{id}/sources. Composes the SourcesTable (editable type dropdown + provenance icon) with the SourceCitationsDrawer drill-down. Wires user-correction mutation via useUpdateSourceClassification.

components.name: SourcesTable
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 2 table of sources cited in a scan. One row per source: name + domain, editable type dropdown, provenance indicator (RuleBased / LLM / You), citation count, platform badges, click-to-open drawer.

components.name: SourceCitationsDrawer
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 2 side drawer drill-down from a SourcesTable row. Lists prompt + AI answer snippet + platform + lens + URL per citation. Lighter-weight precursor to the full Prompt Evidence view from ADR-004 which awaits Findings.

components.name: PromptAnswerHistoryDrawer
components.notes: Row-click drawer on the AI Questions surface. Drills into one question's AI answer history across in-window scans with title pattern Answer history: {AI question} and a compact answer/platform/brand-mention subtitle. Per-answer cards show platform, scan recency, brand-mentioned state, dominant sentiment, brand mention count, first-mention position, evidence snippet, and full answer text. Backed by GET /api/prompts/{promptId}/answers; the BE returns 200 + empty body for prompts outside the caller's workspace so the drawer renders a dedicated not-in-scope state without branching on errors.

components.name: ShareOfVoiceCard
components.notes: Donut of mention-count share per entity (brands + competitors). Used on /overview and /competitors. Optional selectedKeys narrows the slices; omitted = no filter.

components.name: RecommendationRateCard
components.notes: Horizontal bar of recommendation-rate per entity. Drops null rates (no mentions at all) silently. Optional selectedKeys narrows the bars; omitted = no filter.

components.name: CompetitiveGapGroupsCard
components.notes: Per-tracked-brand mention-gap bars vs each in-scope competitor. Positive = brand ahead, negative = competitor ahead. Used on /overview and /competitors. Optional selectedKeys narrows both the group list and the per-group competitor list.

components.name: BrandVsCompetitorCard
components.notes: Horizontal bar of raw mention counts per entity. Complements ShareOfVoiceCard (share vs absolute). Used on /overview and /competitors. Optional selectedKeys narrows the bars; omitted = no filter.

components.name: DataTable
components.notes: Generic sortable data table built on @tanstack/react-table v8. Columns declare their own header / cell renderers via ColumnDef, so custom row content (badges, dropdowns, external-link cells) lives inside the column definition rather than inline. Click-to-sort with the standard three-state cycle (asc → desc → unsorted), aria-sort attribute on header buttons. Column meta accepts `{ align: 'left' | 'right' }` for right-aligned numeric columns (tabular-nums applied), plus `className` / `cellClassName` for fine-grained styling. Migrating page tables to this molecule from raw <table> markup.

components.name: DomainTypesCard
components.notes: Source-type donut — citations broken out by the dominant SourceType per Source. Workspace-aggregate input on /overview (pre-aggregated by the competitive endpoint); on /sources the same DomainTypeShareDto[] shape is computed locally from the filtered domain rows so the donut respects the page's filter bar.

components.name: CoMentionLandscapeCard
components.notes: Per-competitor count of in-scope answers where a tracked brand AND that competitor were both mentioned, plus a per-row 'share of competitor's mentions' caption. Surfaces substitute clusters the buyer is comparing against. Used on /overview and /competitors. Optional selectedKeys narrows by competitor id.

components.name: ScanTopicsScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 3 Topic view list at /scans/{id}/topics. Pivots Topic-scope ScanMetric rows into per-topic table; row click navigates to topic detail route.

components.name: ScanClaimsScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 measurement-model expansion item #14 — factual-claim review inbox at /scans/{id}/claims. Lists every check-able claim the AI made about the brand on this scan with subject/asserted-value, polarity-coded verifiability badge, and a review-status pill (Pending / Verified / Disputed). Filter buttons re-query the BE with `?reviewStatus=` so the list stays cheap; the future Verify/Dispute mutation lives in the same screen but is deferred to a follow-up slice.

components.name: TopicsTable
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 3 table of topics for a scan. Per-row metric pivot (mention rate, recommendation rate, share of voice, avg rank, citations, owned share, dominant sentiment badge). Renders em-dash for null rates so missing-data is distinguishable from a real zero.

components.name: ScanTopicDetailScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 3 Topic detail at /scans/{id}/topics/{topicId}. Three sections: topic metrics card (8 metric tiles + sentiment badges), per-platform breakdown table (runtime Topic×Platform sub-aggregation from backend), top cited sources within the topic.

components.name: ScanCompetitorsScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 4 Competitor view list at /scans/{id}/competitors. Pivots Competitor-scope ScanMetric rows into per-competitor table; row click drills into detail.

components.name: CompetitorsTable
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 4 table of tracked competitors for a scan. Columns: name + domain, mention count, mention rate, recommendation count, recommendation rate. Em-dash for null rates so missing-denominator is visually distinct from a real zero.

components.name: ScanCompetitorDetailScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 Slice 4 Competitor detail at /scans/{id}/competitors/{competitorId}. Metric tile section (mentions, rec rate) plus a table of sources cited on answers that mentioned this competitor (citations joined with answers carrying the competitor mention).

components.name: WorkspaceOverviewScreen
components.notes: Phase 4 v3 default landing surface at /overview. Workspace-scoped: aggregates the last N days of scans across every Brand and TrackerConfiguration. Hero + multi-line trend + Top Entities (Slice A) + competitive sections (Slice B: SoV, gap groups per tracked brand, rec rate, brand-vs-competitor, mention distribution radar, top domains, domain types) + depth sections (Slice C: mentions-by-platform, sentiment, activity heatmap (90d capped), topic coverage, recent chats + drawer). Brand selector multi-select filters every comparison chart; share charts recompute denominators.

components.name: CoreMetricsRow
components.notes: Horizontal row of core visibility metric cards.

components.name: PromptEvidenceTable
components.notes: Table of prompts with corresponding AI answers and confidence.

components.name: FullAnswerDrawer
components.notes: Drawer displaying the full AI-generated answer text.

components.name: TopicPerformanceRow
components.notes: Topic-level performance display with metrics per topic.

components.name: CompetitorPerformanceRow
components.notes: Competitor comparison display showing relative performance.

components.name: SourceCitationRow
components.notes: Source and citation tracking display table.

components.name: RecentScanRunsTable
components.notes: Table of recent scan runs with status and summary data.
